m	Z	
z|Weak reference support for Python.
This module is an implementation of PEP 205:
https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0205/

WeakMethod
finalizec
    A custom `weakref.ref` subclass which simulates a weak reference to
    a bound method, working around the lifetime problem of bound methods.
    )

Mapping class that references values weakly.
    Entries in the dictionary will be discarded when no strong
    reference to the value exists anymore

z WeakValueDictionary.__deepcopy__Nc
defaultr>
WeakValueDictionary.getc
WeakValueDictionary.itemsc
keys
WeakValueDictionary.keysc
Return an iterator that yields the weak references to the values.
        The references are not guaranteed to be 'live' at the time
        they are used, so the result of calling the references needs
        to be checked before being used.  This can be used to avoid
        creating references that will cause the garbage collector to
        keep the values around longer than needed.
        N
valuesrS
itervaluerefs
z!WeakValueDictionary.itervaluerefsc
WeakValueDictionary.valuesc
popitem)
WeakValueDictionary.popitemc
argsrN
WeakValueDictionary.popc

WeakValueDictionary.setdefaultc
hasattr
dictr^
kwargsrI
WeakValueDictionary.updatec
Return a list of weak references to the values.
        The references are not guaranteed to be 'live' at the time
        they are used, so the result of calling the references needs
        to be checked before being used.  This can be used to avoid
        creating references that will cause the garbage collector to
        keep the values around longer than needed.
        )
listr=
	valuerefs.
WeakValueDictionary.valuerefsc
__ior__<
WeakValueDictionary.__ior__c
_collections_abc
Mappingr`

WeakValueDictionary.__or__c
__ror__G
WeakValueDictionary.__ror__)
__copy__rd
__iter__rm
Specialized reference that includes a key corresponding to the value.
    This is used in the WeakValueDictionary to avoid having to create
    a function object for each key stored in the mapping.  A shared
    callback object can use the 'key' attribute of a KeyedRef instead
    of getting a reference to the key from an enclosing scope.
    
KeyedRef.__new__c
KeyedRef.__init__)
d!d"
d#d$
d%d&
d3d'd(
d4d)d*
d+d,
d-d.
d/d0
 Mapping class that references keys weakly.
    Entries in the dictionary will be discarded when there is no
    longer a strong reference to the key. This can be used to
    associate additional data with an object owned by other parts of
    an application without adding attributes to those objects. This
    can be especially useful with objects that override attribute
    accesses.
    Nc
z*WeakKeyDictionary.__init__.<locals>.removeF)
_dirty_lenrD
WeakKeyDictionary.__init__c
z"WeakKeyDictionary._commit_removalsc
.0rh
<listcomp>
z5WeakKeyDictionary._scrub_removals.<locals>.<listcomp>F)
_scrub_removals
z!WeakKeyDictionary._scrub_removalsc
WeakKeyDictionary.__delitem__c
WeakKeyDictionary.__getitem__c
WeakKeyDictionary.__len__c
WeakKeyDictionary.__repr__c
WeakKeyDictionary.__setitem__c
WeakKeyDictionary.copyc
WeakKeyDictionary.__deepcopy__c
WeakKeyDictionary.getc
WeakKeyDictionary.__contains__c
WeakKeyDictionary.itemsc
WeakKeyDictionary.keysc
WeakKeyDictionary.valuesc
Return a list of weak references to the keys.
        The references are not guaranteed to be 'live' at the time
        they are used, so the result of calling the references needs
        to be checked before being used.  This can be used to avoid
        creating references that will cause the garbage collector to
        keep the keys around longer than needed.
        )
keyrefs
WeakKeyDictionary.keyrefsc
WeakKeyDictionary.popitemc
WeakKeyDictionary.popc
WeakKeyDictionary.setdefaultc
WeakKeyDictionary.updatec
WeakKeyDictionary.__ior__c
WeakKeyDictionary.__or__c
WeakKeyDictionary.__ror__)
Class for finalization of weakrefable objects
    finalize(obj, func, *args, **kwargs) returns a callable finalizer
    object which will be called when obj is garbage collected. The
    first time the finalizer is called it evaluates func(*arg, **kwargs)
    and returns the result. After this the finalizer is dead, and
    calling it just returns None.
    When the program exits any remaining finalizers for which the
    atexit attribute is true will be run in reverse order of creation.
    By default atexit is true.
    r
finalize._Info)
weakrefr&
atexit
indexN)
_Info6
_registered_with_atexitr
register
	_exitfuncr
next
_index_iterr
	_registry
_dirty)
infor
finalize.__init__Nc
zZIf alive then mark as dead and return func(*args, **kwargs);
        otherwise return NoneN)
	_shutdownr&
finalize.__call__c
z^If alive then mark as dead and return (obj, func, args, kwargs);
        otherwise return NoneN)
detachQ
finalize.detachc
zMIf alive then return (obj, func, args, kwargs);
        otherwise return NoneN)
peekY
finalize.peekc
Whether finalizer is alive)
alivea
finalize.alivec
z*Whether finalizer should be called at exit

finalize.atexitc
